holdings.infoBox: EWI is an equity ETF with a total of 32 individual holdings. The top holdings are UniCredit stock at 15.99%, Intesa Sanpaolo at 12.43%, Enel SpA at 10.96%, Eni S.p.A. at 4.91%, and Assicurazioni Generali at 4.71%.

overview.description: The iShares MSCI Italy ETF (EWI) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MSCI Italy 25-50 index, a market-cap-weighted index of Italian companies excluding small-caps EWI was launched on Mar 12, 1996 and is issued by BlackRock.

data.title: Too Concentrated in U.S. Stocks? Here's a New Way to Diversify
data.text: Joe Grogan breaks down how Europe's renewed defense spending cycle may create a fresh opportunity for investors looking beyond U.S. markets.

data.title: Italy business lobby calls for emergency action to boost renewables roll out
data.text: Italy's business lobby Confindustria has called for emergency action from the government to speed up the roll-out of renewable energy in the country, which ​is grappling with much higher power costs t...

data.title: Eurozone Government Bond Yields Fall After U.S.-Iran Deal
data.text: Yields on eurozone government bonds declined as investors lowered their expectations for the ECB raising rates after the U.S. and Iran reached an agreement to end conflict.

data.title: Exclusive: Italy working on new inflation-linker for institutional investors, debt chief says
data.text: Italy is working on a new bond aimed exclusively at institutional investors to tap growing demand for ‌inflation-linked products among banks, funds and insurers, the Treasury's debt chief said.

data.text: Italy will ​ramp up its official defence budget this year, but NATO allies should rethink military priorities ‌as warfare is transformed by drones, satellites and data, Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni s...

data.text: The European Central Bank raised interest rates for the first time in almost three years, concluding it can no longer ignore the upswing in inflation caused by the Iran war. The deposit rate was lifte...

data.title: The ECB became the first major central bank to raise rates to tackle a war-driven resurgence in inflation
data.text: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz and ensuing jump in energy prices has dramatically altered the paths for central banks around the world.

data.title: ECB Rate Hike Looks Likely as Inflation Rises. The Fed Could Follow.
data.text: The European Central Bank is expected to raise interest rates for the first time since 2023 as inflation accelerates, a move that could offer clues about the Federal Reserve's next steps.

data.title: Italy to assess best offer as it seeks Monte Paschi exit, economy minister says
data.text: Italy needs to cut its remaining stake ​in bailed-out Monte dei ‌Paschi di Siena (MPS) and will assess which party ​makes the best offer, ​Economy Minister Giancarlo Giorgetti ⁠said on Wednesday.

data.title: Energy prices take center stage as the ECB prepares to decide on rates
data.text: The European Central Bank is expected to raise interest rates on Thursday, amid spiking energy prices. The euro zone is a major energy importer, making it particularly vulnerable to elevated oil price...

data.title: ECB to Keep Guard Up Despite Muted Signs of Inflation Spiral
data.text: Alongside an anticipated interest-rate hike on Thursday, the central bank is expected to raise its inflation forecast from March, which assumed a swift end to the war in Iran.

data.title: Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: U.S. Inflation Data Due, ECB Likely to Raise Rates
data.text: U.S. inflation data in the coming week will be closely watched as investors gauge how likely it is that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates this year.

data.title: Eurozone Economy Shrank in First Quarter as Iran War Darkens Outlook Ahead
data.text: GDP in the currency area fell 0.2% in the first three months of 2026, driven by output declines in Ireland and France.

data.title: EU delays bank risk capital framework by three years, awaiting US, standards
data.text: The European Commission will delay the introduction of a new market risk capital framework for banks for ​three years to see how the U.S. and Britain ‌implement the same international standards, it sa...

data.title: Eurozone Retail Sales Fell in April
data.text: Eurozone retail sales fell more than expected in April as rising energy prices continued to erode consumer spending power.

data.title: EU could lose 1.3 million jobs due to energy price surge linked to Iran war, Commission says
data.text: European automotive, construction, metals, chemicals and transport sectors could lose ​up to 1.3 million jobs ‌this year as a result of a surge in energy prices caused by the U.S.-Iran ​conflict, Labo...

data.title: European shares head for negative open as White House proposes fresh tariffs on EU
data.text: European stocks dipped on Wednesday, as investors weighed U.S. proposals for sweeping new tariffs on 60 countries.

data.title: FTSE 100 rises as mining stocks rally on stronger metal prices

data.title: Eurozone Inflation Climbs Further as ECB Weighs Rate Hike
data.text: Inflation rose to 3.2% in May, a fourth-straight increase and the highest level since September 2023.

data.title: European stocks poised to rebound ahead of key inflation update
data.text: Investors are awaiting data that will shed further light on the economic impact of the U.S.-Iran war.
